description_long: This is a large, freestanding kitchen or dining room cabinet with a two-tiered structure. The upper section features four doors, with two solid panel doors on the left and two glass-panel doors on the right, as well as five circular cutouts likely intended for bottle storage. The lower section consists of three doors and two drawers, all with visible handles, providing ample enclosed storage space. The cabinet has a dark brown top surface on both the upper and lower sections, contrasting with the light gray or off-white body. The overall design is traditional, with arched paneling on the doors and a robust, rectangular form. The object appears to be constructed primarily from wood, with some metal hardware and glass elements.
description: A large, two-tiered freestanding cabinet with dark brown tops, light gray body, glass doors, and bottle storage cutouts. Features multiple doors, drawers, and traditional paneling.
description_view_0: Front view showing all doors, drawers, handles, glass panels, and bottle storage cutouts.
description_view_1: Left side view showing the cabinet's depth and the stepped profile between upper and lower sections.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, flat back with no visible features or openings.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, highlighting the stepped profile and solid side panels.
